summary.text: The question is: "Teen acne treatment options Katy Texas". This seems like someone looking for acne treatment options for teens in Katy, Texas. They want local dermatologists, clinics, or specific tre...

action.query: best dermatologists acne Katy TX teens

content.text:
**Several dermatology clinics in Katy, Texas, specialize in teen acne treatment**, offering personalized plans that range from topical medications and skincare regimens to advanced options like oral antibiotics or isotretinoin (Accutane) for moderate-to-severe cases.[[1]](https://cincoderm.com/conditions/acne)[[2]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/blog-village-dermatology/teen-acne-success-story-month-5-of-accutane-treatment-shows-excellent-progress)

Teens often benefit from early intervention by a board-certified dermatologist to prevent scarring and address hormonal or inflammatory acne common in adolescence. Always consult a professional for tailored advice, as treatments depend on acne type/severity, skin type, and medical history.

### Recommended Katy-Area Clinics for Teen Acne
Here are some well-regarded local options based on their focus on acne (including for adolescents) and patient services:

- **Cinco Ranch Dermatology** (Katy, TX): Board-certified dermatologists provide expert acne care for all ages, with personalized plans from basic regimens to advanced therapies like Accutane.[[3]](https://cincoderm.com/)[[1]](https://cincoderm.com/conditions/acne)
- **Village Dermatology** (Katy office: 23960 Katy Freeway, Suite 230, Katy, TX 77494; also Houston locations): Specializes in teen acne with success stories using Accutane/isotretinoin. They follow a step-wise approach: topicals first, then combinations with orals, hormonal therapy (for females), or Accutane for severe/scarring cases. Phone: 713-952-8400.[[2]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/blog-village-dermatology/teen-acne-success-story-month-5-of-accutane-treatment-shows-excellent-progress)[[4]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/acne)
- **Dermatology Associates of Katy** (23510 Kingsland Boulevard, Suite 110, Katy, TX 77494): Treats acne among other conditions; contact Dr. Uyen Ngoc Mui or the practice at (281) 400-1224.[[5]](https://www.dermofkaty.com/acne/)
- **Texas Dermatology Specialists** (21800 Katy Fwy #200, Katy, TX 77449): Offers advanced medical dermatology for patients ages 3+, including acne regimens with medical-grade products. Phone: (713) 771-1100.[[6]](https://texasdermatology.com/2020/06/17/help-your-teen-eliminate-acne/)
- **Clear Dermatology** (Katy area, near I-10 and Barker Cypress): Comprehensive care for acne and other skin issues in children and adults; call 832-772-3330.[[7]](https://cleardermatology.com/)
- **Katy Dermatology**: Multiple Katy offices; experienced with pediatric and acne patients.[[8]](https://www.katydermatology.com/doctors.html)

Other mentions include medical spas or specialized programs (e.g., acne bootcamps or laser therapies) for milder cases or adjunct care.[[9]](https://anitasskincareclinickaty.com/acne-bootcamp/)

### Common Treatment Options for Teen Acne
Dermatologists typically use a stepwise approach:
- **Mild acne**: Gentle cleansers, benzoyl peroxide, salicylic acid, or retinoid topicals; medical-grade skincare (e.g., EltaMD or SkinCeuticals).[[6]](https://texasdermatology.com/2020/06/17/help-your-teen-eliminate-acne/)
- **Moderate**: Add topical or oral antibiotics.
- **Severe/nodulocystic or scarring**: Consider isotretinoin (Accutane), which requires monitoring but can be highly effective for teens.[[2]](https://www.villagedermatologyhouston.com/blog-village-dermatology/teen-acne-success-story-month-5-of-accutane-treatment-shows-excellent-progress)
- Adjuncts: Hormonal therapies (for females), lasers/pulsed light, extractions, or chemical peels at some practices.[[10]](https://aboutfaceandbodykaty.com/services/laser-procedures/acne-treatment-katy-tx)

Lifestyle factors like diet, stress, and consistent routines also play a role, though professional guidance is key.

**Next steps**: Search for reviews on sites like Yelp or Healthgrades, verify insurance, and schedule consultations. Many practices accept new patients and offer pediatric/teen expertise. If acne is impacting mental health or causing scarring, seek care promptly. This is not medical advice—see a qualified dermatologist for evaluation.
